title: Industrial Policy for an Age of Uncertainty
content: With industrial policy back in fashion worldwide, the question is not whether governments should intervene (they already do), but how they should proceed when they do not know in advance what will work. The answer lies in directed improvisation: creating the conditions for experimentation where it counts.
snippet: With industrial policy back in fashion worldwide, the question is not whether governments should intervene (they already do), but how they should proceed when they do not know in advance what will wor
creator: Yuen Yuen Ang
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is China’s Confidence Justified?
content: China’s political and intellectual elites increasingly see the country’s economic transformation as evidence that state-led development has succeeded where liberal capitalism has failed. Yet structural problems, including overcapacity and persistent inequality, suggest that triumphalism may be premature.
snippet: China’s political and intellectual elites increasingly see the country’s economic transformation as evidence that state-led development has succeeded where liberal capitalism has failed. Yet structura
creator: Jayati Ghosh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Policy Pivot Trump Needs
content: As November’s midterm elections approach, US President Donald Trump must stop pushing for a weaker dollar, which will exacerbate the affordability pressures many Americans face and harm the country’s fiscal position. Fortunately, there are several policy instruments that could help the administration support domestic manufacturing.

creator: Glenn Hubbard
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Whose Vote Will Count in America’s Midterms?
content: Democrats and Republicans alike are more focused on giving themselves an electoral advantage through redistricting than on appealing to voters, because both parties lack a credible vision for middle- and working-class Americans. Without an attractive program, all that is left are tactics.
snippet: Democrats and Republicans alike are more focused on giving themselves an electoral advantage through redistricting than on appealing to voters, because both parties lack a credible vision for middle-
creator: Reed Galen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How to Build a Sovereign AI Stack
content: Paradoxically, no economy can build the architecture of AI sovereignty alone. Coalitions of countries must work together to regulate the sector, design and operate the rails, open the model layer, and universalize the agent interface, thereby embedding contestability and interoperability into every layer.
snippet: Paradoxically, no economy can build the architecture of AI sovereignty alone. Coalitions of countries must work together to regulate the sector, design and operate the rails, open the model layer, and
creator: Jayant Sinha
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Gender Inequality Still Haunts the Economy
content: Why do gender wage gaps and other disparities persist despite converging education levels and mounting evidence that the public opposes such forms of inequality? The problem lies less in outdated beliefs and biases than in entrenched structural constraints to women's economic advancement.
snippet: Why do gender wage gaps and other disparities persist despite converging education levels and mounting evidence that the public opposes such forms of inequality? The problem lies less in outdated beli
creator: Eleni  Yitbarek
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Affordability Crisis Is About More than Prices
content: Too many governments around the world are responding to affordability concerns by treating the symptoms rather than the underlying causes. Negotiating marginal price reductions with retailers might create the appearance of responsiveness, but the results will inevitably disappoint.
snippet: Too many governments around the world are responding to affordability concerns by treating the symptoms rather than the underlying causes. Negotiating marginal price reductions with retailers might cr
creator: Carolina Alves
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: NATO Must Die
content: Some claim that the threats Europe is facing, especially after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, can create the momentum toward political union that the euro crisis and then the pandemic failed to generate. Right or wrong, one thing is clear: a functional defense union requires political union, and NATO's existence is inimical to it.
snippet: Some claim that the threats Europe is facing, especially after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, can create the momentum toward political union that the euro crisis and then the pandemic failed to generat
creator: Yanis Varoufakis
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Cultural Component of National Security
content: Since Russia’s full-scale invasion four years ago, Ukraine has become the world’s most consequential defense laboratory, trialing innovations that can strengthen European resilience. But the country has also demonstrated that national security depends as much on cultural identity as on military strength.
snippet: Since Russia’s full-scale invasion four years ago, Ukraine has become the world’s most consequential defense laboratory, trialing innovations that can strengthen European resilience. But the country h
creator: Tetyana Berezhna
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Keynes, Minsky, and the Economics of Uncertainty
content: Although Hyman Minsky’s version of John Maynard Keynes’s economics had a minimal impact on the mainstream during his own lifetime and in the years following, its central insights remain as relevant as ever. Both men understood that economic theory cannot get away with ignoring the fact of uncertainty.
snippet: Although Hyman Minsky’s version of John Maynard Keynes’s economics had a minimal impact on the mainstream during his own lifetime and in the years following, its central insights remain as relevant as
creator: William H. Janeway
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is Trump’s Iran War a Crime of Aggression?
content: America’s war on Iran is widely considered an illegal act of aggression in clear violation of the United Nations Charter. But assessing whether the war's planners should face prosecution raises difficult questions, largely owing to ongoing uncertainties about the meaning and scope of criminal aggression.
snippet: America’s war on Iran is widely considered an illegal act of aggression in clear violation of the United Nations Charter. But assessing whether the war's planners should face prosecution raises diffic
creator: Lawrence Douglas
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Coming Vote for Europe’s Soul
content: The principle of non-discrimination between trading partners has underpinned international commerce for eight decades. Now, the same principle must guide European lawmakers as they decide whether to ratify the trade agreement reached last year between European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and US President Donald Trump.
snippet: The principle of non-discrimination between trading partners has underpinned international commerce for eight decades. Now, the same principle must guide European lawmakers as they decide whether to r
creator: Kevin Hjortshøj O'Rourke
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Are China’s Young People Fed Up?
content: Decades of misguided economic policymaking have left China in a demographic hole that it seems incapable of climbing out of. As the “world’s factory,” the country is churning out everything except the people whom it will need to sustain its economic development and social stability over the long term.
snippet: Decades of misguided economic policymaking have left China in a demographic hole that it seems incapable of climbing out of. As the “world’s factory,” the country is churning out everything except the
creator: Yi Fuxian
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Myth of the Deserving Billionaire
content: While 55% of Americans say their financial situation is deteriorating, the wealth of the top 1% is reaching record highs. Against this backdrop, defending billionaires from “oppressive” taxation is politically convenient nonsense.
snippet: While 55% of Americans say their financial situation is deteriorating, the wealth of the top 1% is reaching record highs. Against this backdrop, defending billionaires from “oppressive” taxation is po
creator: Teresa Ghilarducci
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Cold Shower for the AI Mania
content: Although generative AI tools have improved rapidly and now outperform humans across many tasks, the market's current euphoria may not be justified. With AI firms increasingly resorting to debt financing, it is worth pausing to consider all the things that could go wrong.
snippet: Although generative AI tools have improved rapidly and now outperform humans across many tasks, the market's current euphoria may not be justified. With AI firms increasingly resorting to debt financi
creator: Raghuram G. Rajan
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Has Taiwan Made Itself Immune to American Betrayal?
content: Although Donald Trump's recent statements about Taiwan seemed like a gift to Chinese President Xi Jinping, such rhetoric no longer tells us as much as it once did. The durability of US-Taiwan relations now rests less on individual leaders than on institutional ties and economic integration, and both have been deepening.
snippet: Although Donald Trump's recent statements about Taiwan seemed like a gift to Chinese President Xi Jinping, such rhetoric no longer tells us as much as it once did. The durability of US-Taiwan relation
creator: Channing Lee
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Trump Sued Himself
content: President Donald Trump has dropped his $10 billion suit against the Internal Revenue Service over the leak of his tax returns in exchange for a $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” to compensate Americans who claim persecution by the previous administration. The point is no longer to conceal corruption but to flaunt impunity.
snippet: President Donald Trump has dropped his $10 billion suit against the Internal Revenue Service over the leak of his tax returns in exchange for a $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” to compensate A
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The US and China Must Learn to Coexist
content: The Beijing summit between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping showed that competition between the United States and China need not become a zero-sum struggle for supremacy. Even as the two countries vie for technological leadership, complete economic decoupling is neither feasible nor desirable.

creator: Pinelopi Koujianou Goldberg
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Gulf’s Post-OPEC Order
content: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC is the clearest expression yet of its desire to break from the Saudi-led regional order. The result could be a more fragmented Middle East marked by intensifying rivalries among Gulf powers, widening proxy conflicts, and chronic instability.
snippet: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC is the clearest expression yet of its desire to break from the Saudi-led regional order. The result could be a more fragmented Middle East marked by in
creator: Rabah Arezki
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Sustainable Economies Will Own the Future
content: The COVID-19 pandemic and the wars in Ukraine and Iran have reframed the issue of sustainability, which is now as much about sovereignty and economic security as it is about planetary health. Countries and companies that fail to recognize this have everything to lose in the coming years.
snippet: The COVID-19 pandemic and the wars in Ukraine and Iran have reframed the issue of sustainability, which is now as much about sovereignty and economic security as it is about planetary health. Countrie
creator: Bruno Bouygues
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can the Climate Crisis Unite Europe?
content: The foundations of Europe’s postwar peace and prosperity are beginning to fracture, yet the European Union still struggles to unite its increasingly divided member states around a common political project. The escalating climate crisis could provide the continent with the shared purpose it has long lacked.
snippet: The foundations of Europe’s postwar peace and prosperity are beginning to fracture, yet the European Union still struggles to unite its increasingly divided member states around a common political pro
creator: Giulio Boccaletti
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Many AI Regulators Has the Pope?
content: Artificial intelligence may be humanity’s greatest opportunity and risk. Where moral leaders have too little influence and states cannot align, actors with system-wide exposure and cross-border reach—in other words, large asset owners—become the only entities capable of imposing needed constraints at scale.
snippet: Artificial intelligence may be humanity’s greatest opportunity and risk. Where moral leaders have too little influence and states cannot align, actors with system-wide exposure and cross-border reach—
creator: Alex Friedman
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Taking Women Farmers Seriously
content: Rather than designing solutions for half the farming population, agricultural researchers, policymakers, and others must consider the specific needs and preferences of women farmers in developing countries. Only then can the huge female agricultural labor force reach its potential to ensure food security and drive economic growth.
snippet: Rather than designing solutions for half the farming population, agricultural researchers, policymakers, and others must consider the specific needs and preferences of women farmers in developing coun
creator: Ruth Khasaya Oniang'o
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: American Decline on Display
content: Donald Trump’s state visit to Beijing has further demonstrated the relative weakness of the United States and the West vis-à-vis the People’s Republic of China and the Global South. While Chinese President Xi Jinping is surely happy with the outcome, Europe now has even more reasons to worry about its future.
